docs.title: fast-vollib
docs.text: fast-vollib is a fast, modern Python library for Black, Black-Scholes, and Black-Scholes-Merton option pricing, implied volatility solving, and Greeks — with pluggable NumPy, PyTorch, and JAX backends.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Three pricing models — Black-76, Black-Scholes, Black-Scholes-Merton
  • Vectorized IV solver — Newton-Raphson with bisection fallback; handles large option chains efficiently
  • Full Greeks — delta, gamma, theta, rho, vega, and get_all_greeks in one call
  • Pluggable backends — NumPy (default), PyTorch (GPU-accelerated), JAX
  • DataFrame-native — price_dataframe works directly on pandas DataFrames
  • Drop-in compatibility — patch_py_vollib() replaces py_vollib / py_vollib_vectorized at runtime
  • Automatic backend selection — prefers CUDA-capable PyTorch > JAX > NumPy

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
import numpy as np
import fast_vollib

# Price a batch of European calls with Black-Scholes
prices = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag=["c", "c", "p"],
    S=[100, 105, 95],
    K=[100, 100, 100],
    t=[0.25, 0.25, 0.25],
    r=[0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
    sigma=[0.20, 0.20, 0.20],
    return_as="numpy",
)

# Recover implied volatility
iv = fast_vollib.fast_implied_volatility(
    price=prices,
    S=[100, 105, 95],
    K=[100, 100, 100],
    t=[0.25, 0.25, 0.25],
    r=[0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
    flag=["c", "c", "p"],
    return_as="numpy",
)

# Compute all Greeks at once
greeks = fast_vollib.get_all_greeks(
    flag=["c", "c", "p"],
    S=[100, 105, 95],
    K=[100, 100, 100],
    t=[0.25, 0.25, 0.25],
    r=[0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
    sigma=[0.20, 0.20, 0.20],
)
# returns a pandas DataFrame with columns: delta, gamma, theta, rho, vega

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Section Description Installation Install fast-vollib with pip, uv, or conda Quick Start More complete worked examples Backend Selection How to choose between NumPy, PyTorch, and JAX Compatibility Drop-in py_vollib replacement guide Benchmarks Performance numbers and how to reproduce them API Reference Complete function signatures and parameters Changelog Version history

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
All public symbols are importable directly from fast_vollib:

from fast_vollib import fast_black_scholes, fast_implied_volatility, ...

docs.title: Common parameters
docs.text:
The following parameters appear across pricing, IV, and Greek functions:

Parameter Type Description flag str \| array-like "c" for call, "p" for put S float \| array-like Underlying spot price F float \| array-like Forward price (Black-76 only) K float \| array-like Strike price t float \| array-like Time to expiry in years r float \| array-like Risk-free rate (continuous, annualized) sigma float \| array-like Volatility (annualized) q float \| array-like Continuous dividend yield (BSM only) model str "black", "black_scholes" (default), or "black_scholes_merton" return_as str "dataframe" (default), "series", or "numpy" dtype numpy.dtype Output dtype; default numpy.float64 backend str "auto" (default), "numpy", "torch", or "jax" return_native bool Return backend-native tensor instead of ndarray; default False

All array-like parameters are broadcast against each other following NumPy broadcasting rules.

docs.title: fast_black
docs.text:
Black-76 model for options on futures.

fast_vollib.fast_black(
    flag,
    F,        # forward price
    K,
    t,
    r,
    sigma,
    *,
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: Option price(s) shaped by broadcasting rules.

docs.title: fast_black_scholes
docs.text:
Black-Scholes model for European equity options (no dividends).

fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag,
    S,        # spot price
    K,
    t,
    r,
    sigma,
    *,
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: Option price(s) shaped by broadcasting rules.

docs.title: fast_black_scholes_merton
docs.text:
Black-Scholes-Merton model with a continuous dividend yield.

fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes_merton(
    flag,
    S,
    K,
    t,
    r,
    sigma,
    q,        # continuous dividend yield (required)
    *,
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: Option price(s) shaped by broadcasting rules.

docs.title: fast_implied_volatility
docs.text:
Solve for implied volatility given a market price.

fast_vollib.fast_implied_volatility(
    price,    # observed market price
    S,
    K,
    t,
    r,
    flag,
    q=None,   # required when model="black_scholes_merton"
    *,
    on_error="warn",   # "raise", "warn", or "ignore"
    model="black_scholes",
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: Implied volatility (σ) shaped by broadcasting rules. Returns NaN for options below intrinsic value when on_error is "warn" or "ignore".

on_error

  • "raise" — raise ValueError for below-intrinsic inputs
  • "warn" (default) — emit a RuntimeWarning and return NaN
  • "ignore" — silently return NaN

docs.title: fast_implied_volatility_black
docs.text:
Convenience wrapper: solves IV under the Black-76 model. Argument order matches py_vollib (price, F, K, r, t, flag).

fast_vollib.fast_implied_volatility_black(
    price,
    F,        # forward price
    K,
    r,
    t,        # note: r before t (py_vollib convention)
    flag,
    *,
    on_error="warn",
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: Implied volatility under Black-76.

docs.title: Greeks
docs.text:
All Greek functions share the same signature:

fast_vollib.vectorized_<greek>(
    flag,
    S,
    K,
    t,
    r,
    sigma,
    q=None,
    *,
    model="black_scholes",
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

docs.title: vectorized_delta
docs.text: First derivative of price with respect to the underlying (∂V/∂S).

docs.title: vectorized_gamma
docs.text: Second derivative of price with respect to the underlying (∂²V/∂S²).

docs.title: vectorized_theta
docs.text: Rate of change of price with respect to time (∂V/∂t). Expressed as daily decay (divided by 365).

docs.title: vectorized_rho
docs.text: Sensitivity to the risk-free rate (∂V/∂r).

docs.title: vectorized_vega
docs.text: Sensitivity to implied volatility (∂V/∂σ). Expressed per 1% move in vol (divided by 100).

docs.title: get_all_greeks
docs.text:
Compute all five Greeks in a single vectorized call.

fast_vollib.get_all_greeks(
    flag,
    S,
    K,
    t,
    r,
    sigma,
    q=None,
    *,
    model="black_scholes",
    return_as="dataframe",
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: When return_as="dataframe" (default), a pandas.DataFrame with columns ["delta", "gamma", "theta", "rho", "vega"]. When return_native=True, a dict mapping Greek name to native array.

docs.title: price_dataframe
docs.text:
Price, compute IV, and compute Greeks for every row of a DataFrame in one call. Supply sigma_col to price options; supply price_col to solve IV; supply both to use the provided values as-is for Greeks.

fast_vollib.price_dataframe(
    df,                           # pandas.DataFrame
    *,
    flag_col,                     # required
    underlying_price_col,         # required
    strike_col,                   # required
    annualized_tte_col,           # required
    riskfree_rate_col,            # required
    sigma_col=None,               # supply to price; result written to "Price"
    price_col=None,               # supply to solve IV; result written to "IV"
    dividend_col=None,            # optional; required for "black_scholes_merton"
    model="black_scholes",
    inplace=False,
    dtype=numpy.float64,
    backend="auto",
    return_native=False,
)

Returns: A new pandas.DataFrame (or None when inplace=True) with computed columns appended. Columns added: "Price" (if sigma_col was given), "IV" (if price_col was given), plus delta, gamma, theta, rho, vega.

docs.title: get_backend
docs.text:
fast_vollib.get_backend(explicit=None) -> str

Return the backend that would be used for a given explicit value (or the auto-resolved backend when called with no arguments).

docs.title: set_backend
docs.text:
fast_vollib.set_backend(name: str) -> None

Set a process-level backend override. Valid values: "auto", "numpy", "torch", "jax".

docs.title: patch_py_vollib
docs.text:
fast_vollib.patch_py_vollib() -> None

Monkey-patch the py_vollib and py_vollib_vectorized namespaces with fast-vollib's implementations. After this call, any code that imports from py_vollib will transparently use fast_vollib. Requires py_vollib to be installed.

See Compatibility for details.

docs.title: Backend Selection
docs.text:
fast-vollib supports three numeric backends. All backends expose the same API and produce numerically equivalent results.

Backend When to use numpy Default; works everywhere; no GPU required torch GPU acceleration on CUDA hardware jax JIT-compiled CPU/GPU/TPU; functional programming style

docs.title: Automatic resolution
docs.text:
When backend="auto" (the default), fast-vollib resolves the backend at call time using this priority order:

  1. Explicit backend= kwarg on the function call
  2. fast_vollib.set_backend(name) process-level override
  3. FAST_VOLLIB_BACKEND environment variable
  4. torch — if torch.cuda.is_available() returns True
  5. jax — if JAX is importable
  6. numpy — always available as the final fallback

docs.title: Per-session (process-level)
docs.text:
import fast_vollib

fast_vollib.set_backend("torch")   # all subsequent calls use PyTorch

# Reset to auto-resolution
fast_vollib.set_backend("auto")

docs.title: Per-call
docs.text:
Every pricing, IV, and Greek function accepts a backend keyword:

price = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag="c", S=100, K=100, t=0.25, r=0.05, sigma=0.20,
    backend="numpy",
)

docs.title: Via environment variable
docs.text:
export FAST_VOLLIB_BACKEND=torch
python my_script.py

docs.title: Inspecting the active backend
docs.text:
print(fast_vollib.get_backend())         # resolved backend for "auto"
print(fast_vollib.get_backend("torch"))  # pass an explicit value to validate it

docs.title: Native tensor output
docs.text:
By default all backends return numpy.ndarray. Pass return_native=True to receive the backend's native type instead:

# Returns a torch.Tensor (float64)
price = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag="c", S=100, K=100, t=0.25, r=0.05, sigma=0.20,
    backend="torch",
    return_native=True,
)

Note

return_native=True has no effect on the NumPy backend — NumPy arrays are already the native type.

docs.title: Backend availability
docs.text: Backend pip install Notes numpy bundled Always available torch pip install "fast-vollib[torch]" CPU wheels are cross-platform; GPU requires CUDA jax pip install "fast-vollib[jax]" CPU-only by default; add jax[cuda13] for GPU

docs.title: Running the benchmarks
docs.text:
# Functional benchmark — pricing, IV, and Greeks throughput
python scripts/benchmark.py

# Accuracy benchmark against a WRDS OptionMetrics dataset
python scripts/wrds_benchmark.py

# Side-by-side comparison with py_vollib_vectorized
python scripts/compare_against_py_vollib_vectorized.py

For the WRDS benchmark you will need access to an OptionMetrics dataset placed at the path expected by scripts/wrds_benchmark.py.

docs.title: Benchmark slices
docs.text: Slice Description Pricing throughput Calls / second for fast_black_scholes at 1 k, 100 k, 1 M inputs IV throughput Solves / second for fast_implied_volatility Greeks throughput Calls / second for get_all_greeks Backend parity MAE between NumPy, Torch, and JAX across all models WRDS accuracy MAE vs. WRDS impl_volatility column on SPX 2023 data

docs.title: Key results
docs.text:
Configuration IV throughput NumPy (compiled bisection fallback) ~10 M solves / s PyTorch (CPU) ~12 M solves / s PyTorch (A100 GPU) ~80 M solves / s JAX (CPU, JIT) ~15 M solves / s

Numbers are approximate and depend heavily on hardware and batch size. Run the benchmark suite on your own hardware to get accurate figures.

docs.title: Accuracy
docs.text:
On a representative SPX 2023 WRDS slice (r = 0.048):

MAE vs WRDS impl_volatility: 1.8e-5  (mean absolute error in vol points)
Max absolute error:          3.1e-4

docs.title: Running the pytest-benchmark suite
docs.text:
uv sync --group bench
uv run pytest tests/ --benchmark-only -v

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented here.

The format follows Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Replaced bare assert guards in price_dataframe with proper ValueError raises (asserts are suppressed under python -O).
  • JAX IV backend: return NaN (not 0.0) for below-intrinsic and zero-price options, matching the NumPy and PyTorch backend behaviour.
  • Removed FASTIV_BACKEND legacy env-var alias (leftover from the fastiv rename); the canonical name FAST_VOLLIB_BACKEND is now the only recognised environment variable. Removed the corresponding test and notebook cell.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• py.typed PEP 561 marker — downstream type-checkers now see the package as typed.
  • Python 3.13 classifier in pyproject.toml.
  • [tool.mypy] configuration block in pyproject.toml.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial public release.
  • Pricing — fast_black, fast_black_scholes, fast_black_scholes_merton with full NumPy vectorization and broadcasting.
  • Implied Volatility — fast_implied_volatility and fast_implied_volatility_black using Newton-Raphson with a compiled bisection fallback (~10 M solves / s on CPU).
  • Greeks — vectorized_delta, vectorized_gamma, vectorized_theta, vectorized_rho, vectorized_vega, and get_all_greeks.
  • Backend routing — pluggable NumPy, PyTorch, and JAX backends with automatic resolution (FAST_VOLLIB_BACKEND env var, set_backend(), per-call backend= kwarg).
  • DataFrame helper — price_dataframe for end-to-end pricing, IV solving, and Greek computation on a pandas.DataFrame.
  • Compatibility — patch_py_vollib() monkey-patches py_vollib and py_vollib_vectorized namespaces at runtime.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Corrected Black-76 forward pricing formula (q = r, not q = 0).
  • Added below-intrinsic NaN guard in the PyTorch IV solver.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Pre-computed CDF symmetry (N(-x) = 1 - N(x)) eliminating 5 redundant CDF evaluations per option in the Greeks hot path.
  • Reduced CDF calls in pricing hot path using the same symmetry identity.
  • Compiled bisection fallback yields a 16× throughput improvement on large WRDS-scale datasets compared to the pure Python fallback.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: fast-vollib is designed as a drop-in replacement for py_vollib and py_vollib_vectorized.

docs.title: What is preserved
docs.text: Aspect Notes Argument order Preserved exactly for all pricing, IV, and Greek entry points flag values "c" / "p" strings work as before return_as="dataframe" Returns pandas.DataFrame, same as upstream return_as="series" Returns pandas.Series, same as upstream Default NumPy output return_as="numpy" returns numpy.ndarray

docs.title: New kwargs (additive only)
docs.text:
fast-vollib adds two optional keyword-only arguments to every function. They are silently consumed when not provided, so existing call sites continue to work without changes.

Kwarg Default Description backend "auto" Override the numeric backend for this call return_native False Return the backend's native tensor type

docs.title: Runtime monkey-patch
docs.text:
patch_py_vollib() replaces the implementations inside the py_vollib namespace at runtime. This is useful for codebases that import from py_vollib directly and cannot be changed.

# At program startup, before any py_vollib imports are used:
import fast_vollib
fast_vollib.patch_py_vollib()

# Now these transparently use fast-vollib under the hood:
from py_vollib.black_scholes import black_scholes
from py_vollib.black_scholes.implied_volatility import implied_volatility
from py_vollib.black_scholes.greeks.numerical import delta

Namespaces patched:

  • py_vollib.black — black, IV, greeks
  • py_vollib.black_scholes — black_scholes, IV, greeks
  • py_vollib.black_scholes_merton — black_scholes_merton, IV, greeks

Requires py_vollib

patch_py_vollib() raises ImportError if py_vollib is not installed.

docs.title: Known differences
docs.text: Behaviour py_vollib_vectorized fast-vollib Below-intrinsic IV Returns NaN silently Controlled by on_error= ("warn" / "raise" / "ignore") Backend NumPy only NumPy, PyTorch, JAX GPU support No Yes (PyTorch + JAX) q in Black-76 Treated as zero Correctly set to r (Black-76 forward pricing)

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or later
  • NumPy ≥ 1.26, SciPy ≥ 1.13 (pulled in automatically)

docs.title: pip
docs.text: pip install fast-vollib

docs.title: uv
docs.text: uv add fast-vollib

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text: fast-vollib ships optional extras for GPU and alternate numeric backends.

docs.title: PyTorch backend
docs.text:
pip install "fast-vollib[torch]"
# or
uv add "fast-vollib[torch]"

docs.title: JAX backend
docs.text:
pip install "fast-vollib[jax]"
# or
uv add "fast-vollib[jax]"

docs.title: Both backends
docs.text:
pip install "fast-vollib[torch,jax]"
# or
uv add "fast-vollib[torch,jax]"

docs.title: GPU (Linux only — CUDA 13)
docs.text:
Installs PyTorch from the CUDA 13.0 wheel index plus JAX with CUDA 13 support:

uv sync --extra cuda

Note: The cuda extra is Linux-only and requires a CUDA 13.x-capable GPU and driver.

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
Clone the repository and install with all optional groups:

git clone https://github.com/raeidsaqur/fast-vollib.git
cd fast-vollib

# CPU-only (default)
uv sync --all-groups

# Both backends on CPU/MPS (macOS or Linux without CUDA)
uv sync --all-groups --extra torch --extra jax

# GPU (Linux)
uv sync --all-groups --extra cuda

docs.title: Optional dependency groups
docs.text:
Group What it installs docs MkDocs + Material theme for building the documentation site bench pytest-benchmark and RAPIDS packages for benchmarking 
uv sync --group docs   # docs only
uv sync --group bench  # benchmarks only

docs.title: Verifying the install
docs.text:
import fast_vollib
print(fast_vollib.__version__)   # e.g. "0.1.0"
print(fast_vollib.get_backend()) # "numpy", "torch", or "jax"

docs.title: Black-Scholes (equity options)
docs.text:
import fast_vollib

# Single option
price = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag="c",      # "c" = call, "p" = put
    S=100.0,       # underlying price
    K=100.0,       # strike price
    t=0.25,        # time to expiry in years
    r=0.05,        # risk-free rate (continuous)
    sigma=0.20,    # volatility
    return_as="numpy",
)

# Vectorized batch — mix scalars and arrays freely
import numpy as np

prices = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    flag=np.array(["c", "c", "p", "p"]),
    S=100.0,                              # scalar broadcasts
    K=np.array([95, 100, 100, 105]),
    t=0.25,
    r=0.05,
    sigma=np.array([0.18, 0.20, 0.20, 0.22]),
    return_as="numpy",
)

docs.title: Black-76 (futures options)
docs.text:
price = fast_vollib.fast_black(
    flag="c",
    F=100.0,   # forward price
    K=100.0,
    t=0.25,
    r=0.05,
    sigma=0.20,
    return_as="numpy",
)

docs.title: Black-Scholes-Merton (continuous dividends)
docs.text:
price = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes_merton(
    flag="c",
    S=100.0,
    K=100.0,
    t=0.25,
    r=0.05,
    sigma=0.20,
    q=0.02,    # continuous dividend yield
    return_as="numpy",
)

docs.title: Implied Volatility
docs.text:
# Recover IV from market prices
iv = fast_vollib.fast_implied_volatility(
    price=np.array([3.63, 6.04, 4.27]),
    S=100.0,
    K=np.array([100, 95, 105]),
    t=0.25,
    r=0.05,
    flag=np.array(["c", "c", "p"]),
    return_as="numpy",
)

# Black-76 IV
iv_black = fast_vollib.fast_implied_volatility_black(
    price=3.5,
    F=100.0,
    K=100.0,
    r=0.05,
    t=0.25,
    flag="c",
    return_as="numpy",
)

docs.title: Individual Greeks
docs.text:
delta = fast_vollib.vectorized_delta(
    flag="c", S=100, K=100, t=0.25, r=0.05, sigma=0.20,
    return_as="numpy",
)

gamma = fast_vollib.vectorized_gamma(
    flag="c", S=100, K=100, t=0.25, r=0.05, sigma=0.20,
    return_as="numpy",
)

docs.title: All Greeks at once
docs.text:
greeks_df = fast_vollib.get_all_greeks(
    flag=["c", "p"],
    S=100.0,
    K=100.0,
    t=0.25,
    r=0.05,
    sigma=0.20,
)
# Returns a pandas DataFrame:
#    delta  gamma     theta      rho     vega
# 0   0.54   0.019  -0.0148   0.121   0.197
# 1  -0.46   0.019  -0.0098  -0.129   0.197

docs.title: DataFrame helper
docs.text:
price_dataframe processes an entire DataFrame in one call. You must supply either sigma_col (to compute prices + greeks) or price_col (to compute IV + greeks), or both.

import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "flag":   ["c", "p", "c"],
    "S":      [100, 100, 105],
    "K":      [100, 100, 100],
    "t":      [0.25, 0.25, 0.50],
    "r":      [0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
    "sigma":  [0.20, 0.20, 0.18],
})

result = fast_vollib.price_dataframe(
    df,
    flag_col="flag",
    underlying_price_col="S",
    strike_col="K",
    annualized_tte_col="t",
    riskfree_rate_col="r",
    sigma_col="sigma",
)
# result has columns: Price, delta, gamma, theta, rho, vega

docs.title: Return formats
docs.text:
All pricing, IV, and Greek functions accept a return_as keyword:

return_as Output type "dataframe" (default) pandas.DataFrame "series" pandas.Series "numpy" numpy.ndarray

Pass return_native=True to get the backend's native tensor/array type (e.g. torch.Tensor when using the PyTorch backend) instead of a NumPy array.

docs.title: Backend selection
docs.text:
# Use a specific backend for all calls in this session
fast_vollib.set_backend("torch")

# Override per-call
price = fast_vollib.fast_black_scholes(
    ...,
    backend="jax",
)

See Backend Selection for more.

docs.title: fast-vollib generated icon assets
docs.text: This directory contains ready-to-use raster exports derived from fast-vollib-icon.svg.

docs.title: Transparent PNG
docs.text:
• fast-vollib-icon-16.png: favicon / browser tab
  • fast-vollib-icon-32.png: small web icon
  • fast-vollib-icon-48.png: desktop shortcut / launcher
  • fast-vollib-icon-64.png: docs / website use
  • fast-vollib-icon-128.png: general app and docs use
  • fast-vollib-icon-180.png: iOS-touch style size
  • fast-vollib-icon-192.png: Android / PWA icon size
  • fast-vollib-icon-256.png: medium app store / docs asset
  • fast-vollib-icon-512.png: high-resolution app / web asset
  • fast-vollib-icon-1024.png: App Store / presentation asset
  • fast-vollib-icon-2048.png: large-format slides / posters

docs.title: White-background JPG
docs.text:
• fast-vollib-icon-512.jpg
  • fast-vollib-icon-1024.jpg
  • fast-vollib-icon-2048.jpg
  • fast-vollib-icon-presentation.jpg

These are useful for PowerPoint, Keynote, Word, and environments that do not handle SVG or transparency well.

docs.text:
• fast-vollib-icon-presentation.png: 1200x1200 transparent presentation-ready asset
  • fast-vollib-favicon.ico: favicon bundle built from 16px and 32px variants

docs.title: Social assets
docs.text:
Files under social:

  • fast-vollib-github-social-1280x640.png
  • fast-vollib-github-social-1280x640.jpg

Use these for GitHub social preview, Open Graph cards, and repository share links.

docs.title: LinkedIn assets
docs.text:
Files under linkedin:

  • fast-vollib-linkedin-post-1200x627.png
  • fast-vollib-linkedin-post-1200x627.jpg
  • fast-vollib-linkedin-square-1080.png
  • fast-vollib-linkedin-square-1080.jpg

The 1200x627 asset is the default landscape post format. The 1080 square asset is useful for carousel or square feed layouts.

docs.title: App and store assets
docs.text:
Files under app:

  • fast-vollib-app-icon-1024.png
  • fast-vollib-play-icon-512.png
  • fast-vollib-play-feature-1024x500.png
  • fast-vollib-play-feature-1024x500.jpg
  • fast-vollib-appstore-portrait-1242x2688.png
  • fast-vollib-appstore-portrait-1242x2688.jpg

These cover a high-resolution icon, a Play Store style feature graphic, and a portrait marketing panel suitable for App Store screenshots or promo slides.

docs.title: Monochrome variants
docs.text:
Files under monochrome:

  • fast-vollib-monochrome-dark-512.png
  • fast-vollib-monochrome-dark-1024.png
  • fast-vollib-monochrome-light-512.png
  • fast-vollib-monochrome-light-1024.png

These are useful for dark-mode favicons, watermarking, one-color print, or places where the full gradient icon is too busy.
